phase1.explanation:
Threads can communicate using wait(), notify(), and notifyAll():

wait():
• Releases the lock and waits
• Must be called from synchronized context
• Waits until notify() or notifyAll() is called

notify():
• Wakes up one waiting thread
• The thread must re-acquire the lock before continuing

notifyAll():
• Wakes up all waiting threads
• Use when multiple threads are waiting

Producer-Consumer Problem:
• Producer produces data, consumer consumes data
• Must coordinate to avoid producing when full, consuming when empty

Key patterns:
• while (condition) { wait(); }  // Always use while, not if!
• Use wait() with conditions to avoid spurious wakeups

⚠️ wait(), notify(), notifyAll() must be called from synchronized context

⚠️ Always use while for condition checking (not if)

⚠️ Spurious wakeups: threads can wake up without notification

Deadlock prevention:
• Always call wait() in a while loop
• Use notifyAll() when unsure
• Keep lock scope minimal

phase2.errorMessage:
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.IllegalMonitorStateException
        at java.base/java.lang.Object.wait(Native Method)
        at Resource.method(Resource.java:5)
phase2.explanation:
wait() is called without having the lock! Or the condition check is wrong.

Two common issues:

1. IllegalMonitorStateException:
   • wait() must be called from synchronized context
   • FIX: Ensure you have the lock

2. Using 'if' instead of 'while':
   • If wait() returns, condition might still be false
   • FIX: while (condition) { wait(); }

Always:
• Call wait() from synchronized method/block
• Use while, not if
• Check condition before and after wait()

phase3.prompt:
Create a simple producer-consumer program:
1. SharedBuffer class with capacity of 5
2. produce(int value) — adds value, waits if full
3. consume() — removes value, waits if empty
4. Create one producer thread (produces 10 items)
5. Create one consumer thread (consumes 10 items)
6. Print each produce/consume action

Use wait/notifyAll for synchronization.
dialogueHints: SharedBuffer: private Queue<Integer> buffer = new LinkedList<>(); private final int CAPACITY = 5;
produce: while (buffer.size() == CAPACITY) wait(); add; notifyAll()
phase3.openingDialogue: Inter-thread communication — talk to your threads! 💬
